top_findings: Missing author bylines and dates on articles hurts E-E-A-T
All images use PNG format — should convert to WebP for performance
Article schema missing author, datePublished, publisher fields
No FAQ/HowTo schema on relevant articles (missed rich result opportunities)
No robots meta tags for indexation control
Homepage title 'Home | FacialCupping.com' is suboptimal for SEO
Some articles are thin content (~150-300 words)
No llms.txt for AI crawler discoverability
quick_wins: Add author bylines and publish dates to article pages
Convert PNG images to WebP format with proper dimensions
Add FAQ schema to FAQ articles for rich results
Improve homepage title tag to be more descriptive

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: Sitemap generated automatically with all 61 pages
robots.txt properly configured allowing all crawlers
Canonical URLs present on every page via BaseLayout
Clean URL structure with logical hierarchy (/section/slug)
HTML lang='en' attribute set correctly on all pages
Static site generation (SSG) — fast, no server rendering overhead
HTTPS-ready with SSL configuration in site URL
Viewport meta tag present for mobile responsiveness

findings.title: No robots meta tags for per-page indexation control
findings.description: No page has a meta robots tag. While the site is fully indexable, this prevents controlling indexation of specific pages (e.g., thin content pages, tag pages).
findings.recommendation: Add meta robots tags to BaseLayout with configurable noindex/nofollow per page.

findings.title: No custom 404 page
findings.description: The site relies on the default Astro 404 page. A custom 404 improves user experience and can guide lost traffic back to relevant content.
findings.recommendation: Create a custom 404.astro page with search, popular links, and navigation.

findings.title: No hreflang tags (minor — single language site)
findings.description: Site serves only English content. No hreflang needed, but self-referencing hreflang is a best practice.
findings.recommendation: Add self-referencing hreflang tag 'en' to all pages for completeness.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Original, well-researched content across 52 articles
Clear topical organization across 10 content sections
E-E-A-T signals present: editorial guidelines, review process, about page
Evidence-based approach with references to research and expert opinions
Good breadth of coverage: learn, benefits, how-to, safety, tools, comparisons

findings.title: No author bylines on any article
findings.description: Articles lack author attribution, which is a critical E-E-A-T signal for Google's content quality assessment.
findings.recommendation: Add author frontmatter field to articles and display author name, bio, and link on each article page.

findings.title: No publish or last-updated dates on articles
findings.description: Google uses publish and update dates as freshness signals. Only the research section lists dates in the homepage display.
findings.recommendation: Add date and lastUpdated frontmatter to all articles and display them prominently in ArticleLayout.

findings.title: Several articles have thin content (< 300 words)
findings.description: Articles like benefits/wrinkles.md and benefits/jawline.md are approximately 150-200 words of body content.
findings.recommendation: Expand thin articles to at least 500 words with more detailed guidance, examples, and citations.

findings.title: Section landing pages lack substantive content
findings.description: Section pages (e.g., /benefits/, /safety/) only list article cards with no introductory content about the section itself.
findings.recommendation: Add 100-200 word introductory content above the article grid on each section landing page.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Title tags follow consistent '<Page> | FacialCupping.com' pattern
Meta descriptions present on every page from Markdown frontmatter
H1 headings present on all pages with clear, descriptive text
Good heading hierarchy (h1 → h2 → h3) throughout
Semantic HTML5 elements (header, nav, main, footer, article)
Breadcrumb navigation on section and article pages
Alt text on all hero/decorative images

findings.title: Homepage title 'Home | FacialCupping.com' is suboptimal
findings.description: The homepage title tag is 'Home | FacialCupping.com' which provides minimal keyword relevance and doesn't describe the site's value proposition.
findings.recommendation: Change to 'Facial Cupping Resource: Benefits, Techniques, Safety & Tools | FacialCupping.com'

findings.title: Homepage meta description is generic
findings.description: Description reads 'The Facial Cupping Resource Center — learn benefits, techniques, safety and tools.' Could be more compelling.
findings.recommendation: Write a more benefit-driven meta description targeting relevant search intents.

findings.title: No Open Graph image on homepage
findings.description: Homepage does not pass ogImage prop, so no OG image is set. This impacts social sharing appearance.
findings.recommendation: Set a default OG image in BaseLayout and pass it explicitly on the homepage.

findings.title: Twitter cards lack image on all pages
findings.description: BaseLayout sets twitter:title and twitter:description but never a twitter:image tag.
findings.recommendation: Add twitter:image meta tag using the same ogImage value for consistency.

findings.title: Section image mapping error: 'tools' points to research.png
findings.description: In [section]/index.astro, the 'tools' section is mapped to '/images/research.png' instead of a tools-specific image.
findings.recommendation: Create a dedicated tools.png image and update the mapping.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: WebSite schema (JSON-LD) on all pages via BaseLayout
Article schema (JSON-LD) on each article page via [slug].astro
Valid JSON-LD syntax throughout

findings.title: Article schema missing required and recommended fields
findings.description: Article schema lacks author, datePublished, dateModified, and publisher fields. This prevents eligibility for Google's rich results (Top Stories, rich snippets).
findings.recommendation: Add author, datePublished, dateModified, and publisher (Organization) to the Article JSON-LD.

findings.title: Missing BreadcrumbList schema
findings.description: Breadcrumb navigation is rendered in HTML but no structured data is provided. BreadcrumbList schema can enable breadcrumb rich results in SERPs.
findings.recommendation: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD to BaseLayout or ArticleLayout using current breadcrumb data.

findings.title: No FAQ schema on FAQ articles
findings.description: Articles like /learn/faq and /safety/faq contain Q&A content but lack FAQPage schema, missing eligibility for FAQ rich results.
findings.recommendation: Add FAQPage schema to FAQ-formatted articles with question/answer pairs.

findings.title: No HowTo schema on technique/tutorial articles
findings.description: Articles like /how-to/beginner and /how-to/techniques contain step-by-step instructions but lack HowTo schema.
findings.recommendation: Add HowTo schema with steps, tools, and time estimates to tutorial articles.

findings.title: No Organization or AboutPage schema
findings.description: The site lacks Organization schema which could improve brand visibility in knowledge panels.
findings.recommendation: Add Organization schema with logo, social links, and contact info to the site.

findings.title: WebSite schema lacks search action
findings.description: WebSite schema does not include potentialAction for site search. Enables Sitelinks Search Box in SERPs.
findings.recommendation: Add SearchAction to the WebSite schema with target URL and query parameter.

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: Static site generation ensures fast Time to First Byte (TTFB)
Minimal JavaScript — only MobileMenu component uses React hydration
CSS is scoped and optimized via Astro/Vite build pipeline
Tailwind CSS v4 with JIT compilation produces minimal CSS bundles
Images use lazy loading on non-hero sections
No heavy third-party scripts (analytics, trackers, ads)

findings.title: All images in PNG format — no WebP or AVIF
findings.description: All 7 public images and 19 article images are PNG or JPG. No WebP or AVIF versions exist. PNG files are significantly larger than modern formats.
findings.recommendation: Convert all images to WebP format with responsive srcsets. Provide PNG fallbacks for older browsers.

findings.title: Images lack explicit width and height attributes
findings.description: No images have width/height attributes set in HTML, which can cause Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) as images load.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit width and height attributes to all img tags, or use CSS aspect-ratio boxes.

findings.title: No resource hints (preload/preconnect) for critical resources
findings.description: No preload or preconnect hints for fonts, hero images, or critical CSS. This delays rendering of above-fold content.
findings.recommendation: Preload the hero image and preconnect to font sources. Preload the primary CSS bundle.

findings.title: Font loading may block render
findings.description: Outfit font is loaded via @fontsource CSS import which triggers a font download. With swap strategy this is acceptable but could be optimized.
findings.recommendation: Ensure font-display: swap is active. Consider preloading the woff2 files for faster first paint.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness (GEO)
what_works: Content is well-structured with clear headings and hierarchy
Article-level descriptions available in frontmatter
Schema.org Article markup present (though incomplete)
Clean, semantic HTML structure throughout
Content organized into clear topical categories

findings.title: No llms.txt file for AI crawler guidance
findings.description: llms.txt is a standard way to help AI crawlers (ChatGPT, Perplexity, etc.) understand site structure. Missing entirely.
findings.recommendation: Create /llms.txt with site summary, key page links, and content overview for AI consumption.

findings.title: Article schema missing fields that AI crawlers use for citation
findings.description: AI models use schema fields like author, datePublished, and description when citing sources. Missing fields reduce citability.
findings.recommendation: Complete the Article schema with all recommended fields to increase chances of AI citation.

findings.title: Passage-level content could be better structured for AI extraction
findings.description: Longer articles lack explicit section summaries or key-takeaway boxes that AI crawlers can easily extract for featured snippets.
findings.recommendation: Add 'Key Takeaways' or summary sections to major articles. Use <section> elements with clear headings.

findings.title: No brand mention signals or external authority amplification
findings.description: The site does not have prominent author bios with links to external profiles (Google Scholar, LinkedIn, etc.) that establish expertise.
findings.recommendation: Add author pages with credentials, links to professional profiles, and publication history.

categories.name: Images
what_works: Alt text present on almost all images
Decorative images correctly use empty alt attribute
Consistent aspect ratio handling via CSS classes
Lazy loading on non-critical images

findings.title: All images use outdated formats (PNG/JPG instead of WebP/AVIF)
findings.description: 7 section images are PNG format. 19 article images are JPG format. Modern formats offer 25-35% smaller file sizes.
findings.recommendation: Convert all images to WebP format. Consider AVIF for even better compression where browser support allows.

findings.title: No responsive image srcsets
findings.description: All images have a single fixed resolution. Mobile users download desktop-sized images, wasting bandwidth.
findings.recommendation: Add srcset attributes with multiple image breakpoints (480w, 768w, 1200w) for all content images.

findings.title: ArticleCard images use data: URLs for article images (missing actual images)
findings.description: In [section]/index.astro, each article in a section uses the same section-level image. Articles lack unique lead images.
findings.recommendation: Add an 'image' field to article frontmatter and display unique lead images per article.

findings.title: No image dimensions set on img elements
findings.description: None of the img tags include width or height attributes, causing potential layout shift as images load.
findings.recommendation: Add width and height attributes to all img tags matching the image's natural aspect ratio.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Add author bylines with names and bios to all articles
Add publish dates and last-updated dates to all articles (frontmatter + display)
Complete Article schema with author, datePublished, dateModified, publisher fields
Convert all PNG/JPG images to WebP format
Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD structured data site-wide
Add meta robots tags to BaseLayout with configurable per-page control

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Add FAQPage schema to learn/faq and safety/faq articles
Add HowTo schema to how-to/beginner, how-to/techniques, how-to/tutorial articles
Improve homepage title tag with descriptive, keyword-rich content
Add Open Graph image to homepage and set default OG image in BaseLayout
Add twitter:image meta tag alongside OG image
Fix section image mapping (tools → tools.png instead of research.png)
Expand thin-content articles (< 300 words) to at least 500 words
Add width/height attributes to all img elements to prevent CLS

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Create llms.txt for AI crawler discoverability
Add Organization schema with logo, social links, and contact info
Add SearchAction to WebSite schema for Sitelinks Search Box
Create a custom 404 page with navigation and search
Add introductory content to section landing pages (100-200 words each)
Create unique lead images per article instead of reusing section images
Implement responsive image srcsets across all content images
Add resource hints (preload/preconnect) for critical assets

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Set up Google Search Console and monitor indexation status
Submit sitemap to Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools
Monitor Core Web Vitals via CrUX and address any regressions
Regular content audits to maintain freshness and accuracy
Build backlinks through guest posts and expert contributions
Add analytics to track organic traffic and user behavior
Implement automated SEO drift monitoring
